g.text: The Masking Bug: Python Integer Overflow vs C uint32_t
Round W[16] | may be OK | if inputs small
Round W[17] | risk starts | sigma on big val
Round W[18] | likely bad | 33-bit sigma arg
Round W[20] | certain bad | cascaded overflow
Final hash | WRONG | all 64 chars off
C uint32_t | automatic | no mask needed | spec-faithful
Python int | unbounded | & 0xFFFFFFFF | easy to forget
JS Number | float64 | & 0xFFFFFFFF | AND coerces i32
Java int | wraps i32 | cast to int | signed — watch
Go uint32 | automatic | no mask needed | spec-faithful
Rust u32 | panics | wrapping_add() | explicit model

text.tspan: /* No masking needed — type enforces it */
/* No masking needed — type enforces it */
# Python: integers are unbounded — MUST mask
# Python: integers are unbounded — MUST mask

div.p: Python produces a valid-looking 64-char hex string — it is just the WRONG hash. No exception, no warning, no indication. Only comparing against NIST test vectors reveals the divergence.
